text.title: Domain Error Hierarchy and ErrorMapper
text.summary: Typed domain error classes, central HTTP translation, and controller error-mapping rule.

section.title: Domain Error Classes

rule.statement: Define a typed AppError hierarchy (NotFoundError, ConflictError, UnauthorizedError, ValidationError); services throw domain errors, never raw Error or HTTP status codes.
rule.rationale: Typed domain errors give the central ErrorMapper precise mapping targets; raw errors produce generic 500s and lose observability.

section.reference: Full AppError hierarchy + central ErrorMapper middleware

rule.statement: Translate domain errors to HTTP responses in a single Express errorHandler middleware; never translate domain errors inside individual controllers.
rule.rationale: Per-handler translation diverges over time; a central mapper ensures consistent status codes, response shapes, and logging across all endpoints.
